page: Microsoft Azure D16ns_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Dnv6 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage capabilities.

Benchmark results place the D16ns_v6 in the average performance tier for most standard operations, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1934.59 and a multi-core score of 15861.01. However, it exhibits strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 31.44 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25 percent. For small-model LLM text generation using a 135M model, it achieves a top-tier result of 404.96 tokens per second, while larger 7B model prompt processing and text generation remain in the average tier. Memory bandwidth is measured at 122.97 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 70.89 GB/sec for write operations.

The lack of bundled local storage represents a key architectural tradeoff, requiring users to attach external network storage for persistent data. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated Intel Xeon processor with advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and AMX, provides solid compute density for general-purpose workloads without the premium cost of GPU-accelerated or memory-heavy instances. This server is suited for web serving, database operations, redis caching, and moderate-scale machine learning inference tasks that leverage CPU-based matrix acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ns_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose server from the Dnv6 family, featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, it includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on external Premium Storage. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates average multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 15861.01, but shows strong capabilities in single-threaded gzip compression at 31.44 GB/sec and top-tier performance in small-model LLM text generation at 404.96 tokens/sec. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is suited for web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference workloads where local storage is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ns_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ns_v6 is a general-purpose server with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and advanced AVX-512 and AMX instruction support.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server with dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix extensions, and optimized network capabilities for standard workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
No bundled local storage, requiring external Premium Storage attachment
Strong single-threaded gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation
Suited for web serving, database operations, and CPU-based inference
